description: Curated source material for the Gokul Rajaraman brain pack — long-form interviews, decision-making frameworks (SPADE), product leadership essays, and angel investing commentary spanning his career at Google AdSense, Facebook Ads, Square, DoorDash, and Marathon Management Partners.

sources.title: Invest Like the Best EP.456 — Gokul Rajaraman: Lessons from Investing in 700 Companies
sources.description: Patrick O'Shaughnessy interviews Gokul on how product building is changing with AI, why companies like Zendesk and Slack are more exposed than Salesforce or NetSuite, sources of defensibility, and lessons from a 700-company angel portfolio.

sources.title: 20VC with Harry Stebbings — Gokul Rajaraman on the 8 Moats of Enduring Software Companies & AI Maxing
sources.description: Deep dive on how to analyse software companies for durability and defensibility in a world of AI, the 8 moats Gokul thinks matter most, why dropouts are 'AI maxing' the world, and why remote early-stage companies are dying.

sources.title: Gokul Rajaraman on Starting a Company in the Age of AI
sources.description: Gokul shares principles and patterns based on his experience helping build Google AdSense, Facebook Ads, and scaling DoorDash and Block — applied to starting a new company in the AI era.

sources.title: Summation (World of DaaS) — Gokul Rajaraman of Marathon: How AI Will Reinvent Ad Platforms
sources.description: Gokul on how AI is reinventing ad platforms, the architecture of OpenAI's ad strategy, attention markets, and the future of monetization on AI-native consumer products.

sources.title: The Aarthi and Sriram Show EP 88 — Silicon Valley Legend Gokul Rajaraman on Founder Mode
sources.description: 82-minute conversation on Founder Mode vs Management Mode, Zuck's micromanagement style, lessons from Google's Larry and Sergey, what makes a great CEO, and why Gokul left Google to launch his own startup.

sources.title: Outlier Academy / Daniel Scrivner #102 — Gokul Rajaraman of Marathon
sources.description: The prolific angel investor on speed, small teams, product management craft, and decision making — plus lessons learned at Square, DoorDash, Google, Coinbase, Pinterest, and The Trade Desk.

sources.title: Square Defangs Difficult Decisions with this System — Gokul Rajaraman on SPADE (First Round Review)
sources.description: Gokul presents the SPADE decision-making framework (Setting, People, Alternatives, Decide, Explain) developed at Google and Facebook, widely deployed at Square and Caviar. The canonical original explainer for SPADE.

sources.title: How to Improve Decision-Making Using SPADE — Gokul Rajaraman on Make Me A Doc!
sources.description: Gokul walks through SPADE with worked examples, explains who the 'decider' is, how to use approvers vs consulted vs informed, and common decision-making anti-patterns to avoid.

sources.title: Prolific Angel Investor Gokul Rajaraman — SPADE for Decision Making
sources.description: Gokul on applying SPADE in startup contexts, when speed beats deliberation, and the costs of consensus decision-making.

sources.title: Gokul Rajaraman — Personal Site & Essay Archive
sources.description: Gokul's personal site with curated essays on being a founder's most helpful and trusted partner, product-led investing philosophy, and angel investing thesis across 700+ companies.

sources.title: Hiring Your First Product Manager — Gokul Rajaraman on Medium
sources.description: The canonical Gokul essay on the biggest mistake CEOs make when looking for their first product manager — what to look for, what to avoid, and how to structure the interview.

sources.title: Gokul's SPADE Toolkit — Canonical Coda Doc
sources.description: The official SPADE template and rationale — published by Gokul. Includes worked examples, decision quality criteria, and embedded forms for teams to run their own SPADE decisions.

sources.title: Gokul Rajaraman — Medium Author Archive
sources.description: Gokul's full Medium archive covering product management, hiring PMs, angel investing lessons, decision-making, founder relationships, and AI's impact on product building.

sources.title: Gokul Rajaraman on Product Thinking and the Future of Innovation — McKinsey
sources.description: McKinsey interview on product thinking as a discipline, what separates exceptional PMs from average ones, and how to set product strategy under uncertainty.

sources.title: #FirstPrinciples with Gokul 'Godfather' Rajaraman — Gojek Engineering Show Notes
sources.description: Long-form interview show notes covering first-principles thinking, Gokul's journey from AdSense to Facebook Ads to Square, hiring lessons, and how to build product teams in high-growth companies.

sources.title: Peak XV — Gokul Rajaraman on Product, Leadership, and Building Enduring Teams (Surge 09 US Immersion)
sources.description: Talk at Peak XV's Surge 09 US immersion on product-market fit, when to opt for generalists vs specialists, and how to build enduring product teams.
